assertions.text: A car pulled out in front of the narrator on the fast road.
Narrator's vehicle was traveling around 110 km/h before braking.
Narrator decelerated from around 110 km/h down to walking pace within a few seconds.
There was no contact between the narrator's vehicle and the other car.
Emergency braking occurred after the other car pulled out in front of the narrator.
After the situation was clear, the narrator accelerated and picked up speed again.
The collision severity was none (no collision occurred).

verdicts.evidence: The claim that "A car pulled out in front of the narrator" is an external visual/third‑party event not recorded by the vehicle sensors; no GPS/IMU channel can confirm another vehicle's maneuver, so this is unverifiable.
The narrator stated ~[110 km/h] but the GPS shows a first recorded speed of [113.0](T2) at t=[0.0](T2), which is within the stated tolerance, supporting the assertion.

Telemetry shows a sustained deceleration detection on [accel_x from t=0.22 to t=4.11 with mean magnitude 0.55 g and peak moving avg -0.7 g](T3) and GPS speed drops of [113.0 to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0](T3), consistent with emergency braking.

GPS speed fell from [113.0 km/h at t=0.0] to [42.0 km/h at t=15.0], and significant drops include [113.0 to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0](T3) and later down to low speeds (min [8.0 km/h](T2)), showing deceleration from ~[113.0 km/h](T2) toward walking pace within a few seconds.

There is no crash impact detection: no high‑g impact spike was detected and the Event Detector reported only sustained decel and speed drops, not an impact; GPS speed remained continuous with no post‑impact signatures and the EDR/telemetry shows no crash-pulse maxima, consistent with [no contact].

The sequence claim that braking occurred after another car pulled out is about the timing of an external actor; sensors show braking starting at [t=0.22](T3) but cannot confirm when another vehicle pulled out, so the ordering is unverifiable.

GPS speed increases after the low point: the first speed sample is [113.0 km/h at t=0.0] and last recorded is [42.0 km/h at t=15.0](T2); while telemetry shows deceleration then subsequent speed rise segments in detect_events (later speed drops and recovery), the statement that the driver "picked up speed again" is supported by GPS showing speeds above walking pace after the event window minimum of [8.0 km/h](T2).

No crash impact was detected by the event detector (no CISS impact spike) and accelerometer peaks are below impact thresholds (peak_abs_dev on accel_x [0.777 g](T2), below impact criteria), consistent with severity being none (no collision).

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a high initial speed of [113.0 km/h](T2), rapid sustained deceleration on [accel_x from t=0.22 to t=4.11 with mean 0.55 g](T3) and GPS speed drops consistent with emergency braking, with no impact spike detected and low peak accelerations ([0.777 g](T2)), so the narrative that the narrator braked hard and avoided contact is consistent with the sensors, while the presence/timing of an external vehicle pulling out is unverifiable by sensors alone.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
